{{Quote|Despite his piracy, it is difficult to truly hate Rus, who as least operates with some style. His is smug, overconfident and roguish. He is a pirate more for the sport of it than the material gain, and his bloodless record is a point in his favor.|[[General/Legends|General]] [[Airen Cracken/Legends|Airen Cracken]]|<ref name="WBC" />}}
'''Beyla Rus''' was a "gentleman pirate" who operated a [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]], along with approximately sixty other beings, who made up the vessel's crew. A [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] from [[Tibro]], he and his ship were active during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]].

==Biography==
Beyla Rus was [[Reproduction/Legends|born]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Tibro]] in [[24 BBY/Legends|24 BBY]]. A [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]], Rus and his crew terrorized the [[Hyperspace route/Legends|space lanes]] for several [[Standard year/Legends|years]]. Styling himself as a "gentleman pirate," his crew was never known to kill anyone and used stun weapons. Rus preferred his victims alive to spread tales of his exploits. During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], Rus was careful not to anger either the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] or [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], preferring to target [[Merchant/Legends|merchant]] [[Starship/Legends|vessels]]. However, with the rise of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], Rus took to raiding their cargo ships. How he obtained information on his targets was unknown, although Rus claimed to be able to small a trap a [[Parsec/Legends|parsec]] away. Rus was charged with theft, piracy, and destruction of New Republic property. The New Republic posted a reward of 15,000 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] for his capture.<ref name="WBC" />

==Personality and traits==
Beyla Rus was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] who was 1.5 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] tall. Although a pirate, Rus styled himself as a "gentleman pirate" who never killed anyone. He was smug, overconfident, and carried himself with a roguish air. Rus' reputation was the most important thing to him, and he reasoned that it was the only thing a pirate had after the loot had been spent. He was an intimidating and persuasive individual, and a competent commander. Rus was known to be a [[Gambler/Legends|gambler]].<ref name="WBC" />

==Skills and abilities==
Beyla Rus was skilled in the use of personal and vehicle-mounted [[Blaster/Legends|blasters]], and trained in both [[Unarmed combat/Legends|unarmed]] and [[Melee weapon/Legends|melee combat]]. He was knowledgeable about languages and [[Star system/Legends|planetary systems]], and had underworld contacts. Rus was capable of flying [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ships]] and [[Astrogation/Legends|navigating]] them through [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]], and effectively using their weaponry. He could also repair the ships and their armament. He was also adept at applying first aid.<ref name="WBC" />

==Equipment==
Rus was armed with a [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster pistol]] and [[Vibroblade/Legends|vibroblade]], and carried a [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]]. He commanded a [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]] crewed by around sixty pirates.<ref name="WBC" />

==Behind the scenes==
Beyla Rus was profiled in [[Wanted by Cracken (sourcebook)|''Wanted by Cracken'']], a sourcebook for ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'' published by [[West End Games]] in [[1993]].
